title: Manuel García (singer-songwriter)

infobox.value: (born 1 March 1970) is a [folk](/wiki/Folk_music "Folk music")-pop Chilean singer-songwriter and guitarist. He has won several awards and has twice received the Chilean National Music Prize, awarded by the Chilean Government (in 2008 and 2012).[[1]](#cite_note-1)

[Presidente Piñera entrego premio a la musica nacional a Buddy Richard, Sergio Sauvalle y Orquesta Sinfonica de la Universidad de Chile](http://www.gob.cl/destacados/2012/01/17/presidente-pinera-entrego-premio-a-la-musica-nacional-a-buddy-richard-sergio-sauvalle-y-orquesta-sin.htm) Gobierno de Chile website, January 17, 2012. Retrieved February 2, 2013.

[Mecanica Popular](http://www.musicapopular.cl/3.0/index2.php?op=Artista&id=241) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20140707004959/http://www.musicapopular.cl/3.0/index2.php?op=Artista&id=241) July 7, 2014, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine") Gabriela Bade, Musica Popular. Retrieved February 2, 2013.
[Musica y luchador social: Manuel Garcia](http://www.subterraneos.com.mx/wp/archives/8732) Andrea Aguilar de Gante, Subterraneos.com. Retrieved February 2, 2013
[Mercat de Música Viva de Vic](http://www.mmvv.net/index.php?lang=1) Retrieved February 2, 2013.

[Manuel García, Pánico](http://www.lamusica.emol.com/detalle/index.asp?idnoticia=271981) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20121024140439/http://www.lamusica.emol.com/detalle/index.asp?idnoticia=271981) October 24, 2012, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine") David Ponce, www.lamusica.emol.com, El Mercurio. Retrieved February 2, 2013.

[Manuel García, Témpera](http://www.lamusica.emol.com/detalle/index.asp?idnoticia=316479) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20121024135400/http://www.lamusica.emol.com/detalle/index.asp?idnoticia=316479) October 24, 2012, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine") Rodrigo Alarcón, www.lamusica.emol.com, El Mercurio. Retrieved February 2, 2013

[Manuel García, S/T](http://www.lamusica.emol.com/detalle/index.asp?idnoticia=414181) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20121024135602/http://www.lamusica.emol.com/detalle/index.asp?idnoticia=414181) October 24, 2012, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine") Marisol García, www.lamusica.emol.com, El Mercurio. He has won several awards and has twice received the Chilean National Music Prize, awarded by the Chilean Government (in 2008 and 2012).[[1]](#cite_note-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

| Manuel García | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Background information | |

| Origin | [Arica](/wiki/Arica,_Chile "Arica, Chile"), Chile |
| Genres | [Folk](/wiki/Folk_music "Folk music"), [trova](/wiki/Trova "Trova"), [acoustic](/wiki/Acoustic_music "Acoustic music") |

Musical artist

**Manuel Javier García Herrera** (born 1 March 1970) is a [folk](/wiki/Folk_music "Folk music")-pop Chilean singer-songwriter and guitarist. He has won several awards and has twice received the Chilean National Music Prize, awarded by the Chilean Government (in 2008 and 2012).[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

García studied History and Geography Education at the [University of Tarapacá](/wiki/University_of_Tarapac%C3%A1 "University of Tarapacá") in [Arica](/wiki/Arica "Arica") and then moved to [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago") in 1994 to study professional guitar performance ("interpretación superior en guitarra") at the [Pontifical Catholic University of Chile](/wiki/Pontifical_Catholic_University_of_Chile "Pontifical Catholic University of Chile") with fellow musician [Luis Orlandini](/wiki/Luis_Orlandini "Luis Orlandini"). His professional career began in 1995 when he founded the band Coré (which also included later members of Chilean band [Inti Illimani](/wiki/Inti_Illimani "Inti Illimani")). However, he left Coré in 1997 and formed his new band [Mecánica Popular](/w/index.php?title=Mec%C3%A1nica_Popular&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mecánica Popular (page does not exist)") with Diego Álvarez, Marco Chávez and fellow Coré member Mario Villalobos.

## Musical career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Musical career")]

Mecánica Popular mixed rock and [trova](/wiki/Trova "Trova") styles and were influenced by the ["nueva canción chilena"](/wiki/Nueva_canci%C3%B3n "Nueva canción"), [trova](/wiki/Trova "Trova"), rock and even poetry. They released three studio albums between 1999 and 2003 and now, though formally disbanded, they often support Manuel García in his solo performances.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Solo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Solo")]

Since 2003, García has worked on several solo projects, one of the first of which was a documentary about the life of Argentinian musician [Atahualpa Yupanqui](/wiki/Atahualpa_Yupanqui "Atahualpa Yupanqui") (2003).[[3]](#cite_note-3)
The same year he was invited to open the festival Mercat de Música Viva de Vic[[4]](#cite_note-4) in [Barcelona](/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona"), Spain.

In 2005, he released his first album, *Pánico* (Panic) in collaboration with two members of Mecánica Popular, Christian Bravo y Diego Álvarez. The album received good reviews in the national Chilean media[[5]](#cite_note-5)

García's second solo album, *Témpera*, was released in 2008 with a sound closer to trova, and rock, with references to [Violeta Parra](/wiki/Violeta_Parra "Violeta Parra") and [Atahualpa Yupanqui](/wiki/Atahualpa_Yupanqui "Atahualpa Yupanqui"). His raw guitar sound recalls fellow Chilean musician [Chinoy](/wiki/Chinoy_(musician) "Chinoy (musician)"), with whom he has shared a stage on several occasions.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

His third album was called *S/T* (2010). Its electric sound (including guitar solos) reveals a variety of influences, though it is considered a simpler album, free of the complex arrangements seen in the previous two.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

His final record to date, *Acuario*, again surprised fans with electronic sounds that sometimes recall [Gepe](/wiki/Gepe "Gepe") or [Javiera Mena](/wiki/Javiera_Mena "Javiera Mena"). *Acuario* has been called a well-accomplished twist on García's signature style, without losing the heart that comes through in his lyrics.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Manuel Garcia is part of the line-up for the third edition of [Lollapalooza](/wiki/Lollapalooza "Lollapalooza") Chile[[9]](#cite_note-9) taking place in Santiago in April 2013.

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Discography")]

### Studio albums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Studio albums")]

* 2005 – *Pánico*
* 2008 – *Témpera*
* 2010 – *S/T* ([Altazor Awards](/wiki/Altazor_Awards "Altazor Awards") winner).
* 2012 – *[Acuario](/wiki/Acuario_(album) "Acuario (album)")*
* 2014 – *Retrato Iluminado*
* 2016 – *Harmony Lane*

### With Mario Rojas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: With Mario Rojas")]

* 1997 – *Musi-cachi-lena*

### DVD

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: DVD")]

* 2011–*En vivo (Live at the) Teatro Caupolicá*

### With Mecánica Popular

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: With Mecánica Popular")]

* 1999 – *Mecánica Popular*
* 2000 – *La casa de Asterión* (nominated for an [Altazor Award](/wiki/Altazor_Awards "Altazor Awards"))
* 2003 – *Fata Morgana*
* 2005 – *En plena luz*
* 2009 – *Mecánica Popular en vivo*

### Other appearances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Other appearances")]

* 2008 – *[Víctor Jara sinfónico](/w/index.php?title=V%C3%ADctor_Jara_sinf%C3%B3nico&action=edit&redlink=1 "Víctor Jara sinfónico (page does not exist)")* (with [Concepción University Orchestra](/w/index.php?title=Concepci%C3%B3n_University_Orchestra&action=edit&redlink=1 "Concepción University Orchestra (page does not exist)"))
* 2008 – *eXile* (Festival Mercat de música Viva de Vic, [Barcelona](/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona"))
* 2010 – *Música libre*, [Los Bunkers](/wiki/Los_Bunkers "Los Bunkers")

### Soundtracks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Soundtracks")]

* 1998 – *Fantasmas de fierro*
* 2003 – *Las condenadas*

### Documentary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Garc%C3%ADa_(singer-songwriter)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Documentary")]

* *[Roberto Parra](/wiki/Roberto_Parra "Roberto Parra")*
* *La [cueca](/wiki/Cueca "Cueca") brava de Nano Núñez*, [Mario Rojas](/wiki/Mario_Rojas "Mario Rojas") documentary about [Los Chileneros](/w/index.php?title=Los_Chileneros&action=edit&redlink=1 "Los Chileneros (page does not exist)").
* *[La ciudad de los fotógrafos](/w/index.php?title=La_ciudad_de_los_fot%C3%B3grafos&action=edit&redlink=1 "La ciudad de los fotógrafos (page does not exist)")*
* *Catalejo*
* *Las horas del día*
* *El mar mi alma*
